title: feat: support AI-generated masks in shape_to_mask
body: Updated the export functionality to support AI-generated masks. The function now handles mask data from JSON files, including AI-generated mask formats (list/array and base64 encoded images).

files_modified.patch:
@@ -8,6 +8,8 @@
 import PIL.Image
 import PIL.ImageDraw
 from loguru import logger
+import base64
+import io
 
 
 def polygons_to_mask(img_shape, polygons, shape_type=None):
@@ -17,7 +19,7 @@ def polygons_to_mask(img_shape, polygons, shape_type=None):
     return shape_to_mask(img_shape, points=polygons, shape_type=shape_type)
 
 
-def shape_to_mask(img_shape, points, shape_type=None, line_width=10, point_size=5):
+def shape_to_mask(img_shape, points, shape_type=None, line_width=10, point_size=5, mask_data=None):
     mask = np.zeros(img_shape[:2], dtype=np.uint8)
     mask = PIL.Image.fromarray(mask)
     draw = PIL.ImageDraw.Draw(mask)
@@ -40,13 +42,23 @@ def shape_to_mask(img_shape, points, shape_type=None, line_width=10, point_size=
         cx, cy = xy[0]
         r = point_size
         draw.ellipse([cx - r, cy - r, cx + r, cy + r], outline=1, fill=1)
+    elif shape_type == "mask" and mask_data is not None:
+        if isinstance(mask_data, (list, np.ndarray)):
+            mask_array = np.array(mask_data)
+        else:
+            mask_img = PIL.Image.open(io.BytesIO(base64.b64decode(mask_data)))
+            mask_array = np.array(mask_img)
+        mask = PIL.Image.fromarray(mask_array)
+        x, y = map(int, points[0])
+        final_mask = PIL.Image.new('L', img_shape[:2][::-1], 0)
+        final_mask.paste(mask, (x, y))
+        return np.array(final_mask, dtype=bool)
     else:
         assert len(xy) > 2, "Polygon must have points more than 2"
         draw.polygon(xy=xy, outline=1, fill=1)
     mask = np.array(mask, dtype=bool)
     return mask
 
-
 def shapes_to_label(img_shape, shapes, label_name_to_value):
     cls = np.zeros(img_shape[:2], dtype=np.int32)
     ins = np.zeros_like(cls)
@@ -58,7 +70,7 @@ def shapes_to_label(img_shape, shapes, label_name_to_value):
         if group_id is None:
             group_id = uuid.uuid1()
         shape_type = shape.get("shape_type", None)
-
+        mask_data = shape.get("mask", None)
         cls_name = label
         instance = (cls_name, group_id)
 
@@ -67,7 +79,7 @@ def shapes_to_label(img_shape, shapes, label_name_to_value):
         ins_id = instances.index(instance) + 1
         cls_id = label_name_to_value[cls_name]
 
-        mask = shape_to_mask(img_shape[:2], points, shape_type)
+        mask = shape_to_mask(img_shape[:2], points, shape_type, mask_data=mask_data)
         cls[mask] = cls_id
         ins[mask] = ins_id

steps.operation: Extend `shape_to_mask` to accept and render AI-generated mask payloads (array/list or base64-encoded image) when `shape_type == "mask"`
steps.reason: Export/import workflows can include AI-generated masks stored in JSON (either as raw arrays or base64-encoded images). The existing implementation only rasterized geometric shapes, so it needed a path to decode/convert provided mask data and composite it into the correct image-space location using the provided top-left `points[0]` offset.
steps.side_effects: Changes the function signature (adds `mask_data`) which may affect any external callers expecting the old signature; introduces decoding/IO dependencies (base64, BytesIO, PIL open) that can raise errors on malformed data; the early return for mask shapes bypasses the previous draw-to-mask flow and may alter behavior if callers relied on prior handling of `shape_type == "mask"`.

steps.operation: Plumb per-shape `mask` data from JSON shape dictionaries into mask generation during label rasterization
steps.reason: Even with `shape_to_mask` supporting `mask_data`, label generation must actually pass the mask payload from each shape entry (e.g., `shape["mask"]`) so that AI-generated mask shapes are correctly converted into `cls`/`ins` arrays during export/label creation.
steps.side_effects: Shapes that include a `mask` field will now take precedence for `shape_type == "mask"`, which can change results compared to polygon fallback behavior; downstream consumers of `cls`/`ins` may see different instance extents for mask-based shapes; any shapes missing `mask` while declaring `shape_type == "mask"` will continue to follow the non-mask codepath and may still assert/fail depending on point count.

step_plan.summary: Adds support for AI-generated masks by allowing `shape_to_mask` to decode mask data from JSON (arrays or base64 images) and updating label rasterization to pass that mask payload through when processing shapes of type "mask".
